expectations.evidence: Property is never set. Lines 54–62 show IdCaptureSettings configuration but rejectExpiredIds is absent. Instead, expiry is checked manually via isExpired() in didCapture.
Property is never set. Age is checked manually via isUnderage() in didCapture rather than using the SDK rejection setting.

expectations.text: Uses a switch statement on the RejectionReason parameter in didReject
Has a case for .documentExpired with a distinct user-facing message about the document being expired
expectations.evidence: No case .documentExpired in the switch. The switch only handles .timeout and default.

expectations.text: Has a case for .holderUnderage with a distinct user-facing message about the age requirement
expectations.evidence: No case .holderUnderage in the switch. The switch only handles .timeout and default.

expectations.text: Has a case for .timeout with a distinct user-facing message about the capture timing out
expectations.evidence: Lines 126–127: case .timeout: message = Constants.Message.timeout — "Document capture failed. Make sure the document is well lit and free of glare. Alternatively, try scanning another document"

expectations.text: Has a default case for all other rejection reasons
Does NOT use rejectHolderBelowAge as a String — it must be an Int literal (21)
expectations.evidence: rejectHolderBelowAge is never used at all. The age check is implemented manually (isUnderage()) rather than via the SDK setting, so the required Int literal assignment is absent.
